title: QUIC CRT detached resumption-ticket carrier handoff architecture
purpose: Describe the smallest honest cross-lifetime ticket handoff seam: export an opaque detached carrier from the owned snapshot on the managed client/runtime path and store it as dormant internal state in a later client setup without changing handshake behavior or public surface.
risks: If the detached carrier is reused as PSK material or fed into ClientHello, the slice will overgrow into resumption infrastructure the repository has not proven.
If later client setup stores the carrier only as a renamed alias for the existing runtime-owned bytes, the slice will not be a real cross-lifetime handoff.
If the carrier changes handshake/bootstrap behavior or opens early data, the slice will overclaim capability it still does not own.
design_summary: The managed client/runtime path already owns a runtime-local opaque resumption-ticket snapshot under REQ-QUIC-CRT-0127, and the bridge/runtime path already surfaces ticket ingress under REQ-QUIC-CRT-0128 and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0129. This slice adds a separate immutable detached carrier that the originating runtime can export by value and that a later managed client setup can accept as dormant internal state. The carrier remains opaque and does not participate in handshake behavior, early-data admission, PSK derivation, or any public surface change.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice covers only detached export and dormant import of opaque resumption-ticket state across client connection lifetimes. It does not add PSK derivation, a ClientHello replay path, 0-RTT packet support, anti-replay, key update, or a public API expansion.
